Things I am happy I no longer tolerate/do: 
- say Sorry needlessly or overexplaining 
- surprise phone calls (Amen! DoNotDisturb)
- letting others’ “urgencies” own my time/headspace 
- unnecessary notifications turned on
- equating “busy” with focus
- equating “busy” with noise
Nearing my 38 years in April and can’t wait to let go of more, more! and make space for the new and actually worthwhile 👌🏽☺️🫖

@roselyddon Last time I napped in public was an airport, day was too long and wasn’t over yet. My particular sensitivities (audio mostly) typically prompt me for a spot somewhere to reduce over-stimulation:
@roselyddon foam earplugs, something to cover my eyes, yoga mat a bonus + timer for 10 - 20 - 30 min = Julia battery recharged (% depends on kind of day).

@roselyddon If I don’t do tend to myself when I truly need it, my inner resources may be lacking so, that I will be irritated, moody, difficulty “masking” (see adhd or autism) and if I can’t keep it “together” because the world is too much sometimes, deep overwhelm may be last to happen.

@roselyddon Sooooo … yeah. 😉
Short periodic naps - inside or outside - are essential for me. I’ve reduced being ashamed of asking for a Pause and Rest. I would love to hear of others finding their balance and self advocating more in public spaces or types of gatherings.

@roselyddon I’ll call it: Rest Against the Machine.

Dear @NotionHQ 

For the longest time, I was lost. Finding myself crushing on different apps whose names shall remain anonymous, I truly thought e/one was the real deal.

My restless creative ND brain zeroed in, restarting new organizational structures and chasing the high of honeymoon app love. 

But then, Notion, I found you.

At first I thought you were like the rest, with a lot of flair and fluff and promises, eventually breaking my heart. 

Then I gave you a chance. And we started dating. It was a very slow build, affected by past app let-downs.
But you were patient. And confident about your worth, which intrigued more and more each time I interacted and discovered new features.

Suffice it to say, that I realize I am fully and truly in love with you and you are my Number 1️⃣  

After all this time, I feel I can finally rest bc I have found a home in you that reflects the most obscure and intricate workings of my mind.

For this, I will venture to take a full leap that does not scare me anymore 

@NotionHQ Will you marry me? 💖

Neuro-spicy Diaries (1.0)

I poured some water to boil for my tea and somehow ended up in the bathroom to record my voice for a song I’m practicing these days (nothing fancy).

I recorded two songs and heard the playback to evaluate. 
When I finally emerge, my nose tells me I better come back to the kitchen. 

I barely made it, most water had evaporated for my tea. Poured some more into pot again. Round 2!

Lesson: never leave my cooking unattended. (Brain: yeah…. That was fun, we gotta do this again sometime)
🫥🤦🏽‍♀️

A thought seed I woke up with recently. There is no heart without art, and viceversa. 

Literally .... "heART"

These two intertwined are what create the wholesome power - a meaningful purpose, a lasting vibration.
To create Art from your heart center … is to complete a cycle that starts with You (expression, integration of experiences) and ends with the World (inclusion, letting go, sharing).

Otherwise, (he)Art is not fulfilling its purpose in lighting up our massive “grid” of human connectedness. And helping one transcend our own illusions of reality. 

It falls flatter, or does not go the amplified distance that was truly called for.

Something about nighttime feels different for me as a writer/expressionist - like I have a unique kind of access to my inner wonderland than I do during the daytime. I don’t think I’m alone in this. 

I’ve taken to calling these approaches my Sun Self and my Moon Self. lol

(Sun/Moon self feels really right to say, as my interest in Tarot reflects deeply). 

So right now, it’s my Moon self here. Hello there ✌🏾

This time for me is v beautiful in a shadowy way. 
I feel way more clearer than during daytime. Less noise. Less ego maybe. I feel more connected to my world inside, even as I’m on TW now.

But it’s like I’m sitting out on the balcony or porch in the quietude, talking to a friend about things we don’t normally let come to surface.

And it’s all okay, really. We’re just sitting here, quietly contemplating this process of living.

Flabbergasted.

Discombobulated.

(This was my favorite word for a long while. The way the word travels while I pronounce it)

Conundrum.

(This one became my new favorite after previous word 👆🏾)

Just words that you are (or have been) drawn to and why. Doesn’t need to be exotic or rare word necessarily.

@dthorson @tasshinfogleman Welcome back. I think it could be possible, yes. ✨ On my end, I’m figuring out a few things that work better for me now than they did before.

@dthorson @tasshinfogleman 1) Create Lists by types of content you normally follow (ex world News - Esoteric - Nerdy - psychology - Local news - etc) These will appear as tabs at top of your Home Screen on Twitter (I refuse to call it the other name lol)

@dthorson @tasshinfogleman 2) Start moving accts youre Following into these Lists they might correspond to  - by their general type of content.
You’ll have to do it one by one, though but can do over course of days as you cross paths with a tweet here/there, adding them to a list accordingly and moving on.

@dthorson @tasshinfogleman 3) When you’ve done a good initial chunk of this task, you can reduce consuming/scrolling under tabs “For You and Following” and move to your Lists depending on what you have energy for.

@dthorson @tasshinfogleman I feel less burned out and able to engage more. Scrolling before jumbled everything together and I didn’t find it healthy, bc I wanted to connect better.

Still, you’ll need to adequately take breaks off TW bc it’s a universe of rabbit holes all linked together, but it’s doable.

An old but timeless poem i wrote. Motivational, this particularly hits different for me in the early morning. 

👇🏾

“RiseFallRise”

.
I.
I can.
I can do this.
I can  be  this.
I can live through it.
I can rise, against all.
I can inspire! the S u n.
I can transform, infinitely.
I can fly, whenever I choose.
I can roll, in the fresh, dewy grass.
I can unfold, from the deepest place of Love.

I can shed, layers and layers, glossy underneath.
I can dig, my hands into this Earth.
I can shape, the Voice inside.
I can step, onto a stage.
I can switch roles.
I can climb!
I can kiss …
I can be
This.
I can.
Do.
I.
.

I took a moment to be outside in my backyard and just breathe after the jam sesh today online, feeling all its music still moving within me.
I stared at the view for the longest time. It's very breezy this season here and it feels perfect. Sky is so clear.

I live literally right next to __xxx__ and that adds a dimension of strangeness, like layers of remembrance. I stared at the beautiful mountains - so big, so high, lush green, and I thought to myself,
“I wanna climb a mountain sometime. I want to see the world from a mountain peak once. I want to feel the victory of a challenging road ahead and rest upon the laurels of my effort proudly.”

I thought of all the ways I've come to where I am, to still say 

"Now, what?" 

I want to shift more boldly but to do *that* I must also travel lighter, within myself, outward as well.

I must learn the art of detaching from what doesn't serve my purpose right now. And … I want so much, and it pains me sometimes to admit I can't be everything for everyone. I want to be it all. But that is not possible in this body. I have to learn to live with that thorn.
✨I am enough. I am worthy and valued as I am. And am becoming.✨

Saturday/am: 20min

Gonna start by saying I’m proud of myself for reminding me to just sit down for another session. 

Something different I’ve been doing for this #pauseinpublic series is to use a stopwatch instead of a timer. It’s been a very interesting switch.
With a stopwatch, I wanted to figure out how long it actually takes me to reach a moment where I’m like “okay, this is it for today, I’m really ready to move on with this day.”

And, I am encouraged to explore further in without feeling like I’m about to lose something bc the timer went off just when I was at a good point. 

(I think for beginners, this might be hard bc inner world can be a huge abyss and navigating it needs some limits to start.)

As for the experience, since I hadn’t sat down for 2-3 days, I felt myself swirling with many thoughts. It took some time for me to settle. Then something shifted for a little bit when I chose to walk behind my thoughts and see them for what they all are: desires or needs.

The work afterwards is to recognize which ones are mine, are true and at my core of living. 

I feel like every sesh has a wealth of experience to share longer than TW allows, but then that is how I know I am truly abundant inside.

Sometimes you just need to be really silly with yourself and call yourself cute names to soften your outlook and move through discomfort. 

My cute nickname is secret … until I live w my future partner and definitely overhear my dialogue. 😄

eye / Can’t believe it took me about 4 whole days and nights to recover from a long socially hyped up weekend. 

Today i feel like myself again, like finally back to 100. Even did yard work for a little bit.

Anytime I get invited somewhere, I run through a mental checklist: what is the vibe I’m going into? 
Am I rested enough already? 
Will I be able to deep talk or is the environment more conducive to light talk? 
Who will be around to support/elevate/stabilize this energy?
Etc.

And most importantly: 
Are they respectful of my needs and main preferences?
Ex. vegt diet and need for short naps for longer days.
I know who my people are when they accept this about me and don’t bully me into keeping up with them. 
At this age (39), I’m so done w pretending✊🏽

Other essential needs are figuring out noise levels of places. Some places just have the worst acoustic design and echo inside. 🫠

But I take my earplugs and headphones just in case. I don’t disguise my sensitivity anymore and  also try to practice some healthy exposure.

This all might make me sound uptight, but that’s okay. I don’t believe I am, and with the adequate enough settings and support to reduce overstimulation, I’m actually pretty cute n fun to have around. Dare I say, unforgettable and adorkable?! 😜💖

@AskYatharth I get it … although it depends on a few specs for me. If I were driving, i can’t converse much and drive at the same time - music helps me relax. (Exceptions are when I know the route well) At least i would take care of the volume and check in w the person who’s riding w me.

At a few points in my life, I did an interesting exercise I thought of: play Be my Own Therapist. 

What would I tell myself?
I write in a journal the first question “she” would ask me, to which the Me who is experiencing struggle has to come up with an answer, even if unsure.

And so it goes back and forth, back and forth, back and forth until I have my own answers - or at the very least, first steps towards dissolving tensions.

The urge to share something online of my inner world and - within a few min or even secs of scrolling - the original point of inspo is gets harder to grasp into a shared post. 

Scrolling (can) kill creativity.

Or it can also add to your fire. 

Some cycles are meant to stay close only to a handful of accounts that give back 💞
Other cycles are meant for you to open your world wider than before, inviting the Unknown, because you trust yourself and your guides for protection when needed.

Either way, my point is Protect Your Attention. What’s meant to become something greater coming from you can be dissolved quickly by an excess of information through scrolling. 
It’s not all your fault though. Apps are made to keep us there.

But we are what we consume the most.

Sundown. I am stirred by the act of feeling. This heart that moves when it thinks of things, moments or people that present definition of love. 
I was made to feel. It can never be suppressed. 
It bubbles up to the surface in the oddest moments, but is never unwelcome.

Sundown. 
I don’t want to turn the lights on just yet. I want to move about in the approaching darkness, with the light of my presence, the knowing of how to navigate this space I call home. 
Now there is barely any light but still I walk. Trusting I’ll move where I am called to.

Listening. Listening a lot nowadays. 
What is the Voice that says “follow me” as I breathe and demonstrate acts of living? 
I want it to be louder. I want it to be faithful. I want it to be stronger. I want to serve this Voice that is in me, devoted to its fierce expression.

Everything is an experiment, if looking through the lens of curiosity. Such are interesting small experiments I do on myself. 
Navigate in the darkness. Dare to appreciate silence for a bit more. 

Nothing is actually silent, can you notice that, too?

So real talk:
This mentality of “5 min” is what I’m applying to almost everything lately. 

Often by default, my mind can go 💯 to the farthest possible outcomes (good, bad, fantastic, etc.). https://t.co/MLYoBqD3S7

And many times the process of starting or continuing is met with the overwhelm of “what can be.”

So, the important thing for someone like me in tasks I may find “too big” are to narrow it down excessively! until I can get myself to say Yes.

This doesn’t mean that every single thing will be done for just 5 minutes. 

The essential premise is just to BEGIN - because in the action, momentum is built and I can experience myself enjoying the process, giving it more than I thought I could.

*sharp intake*

Kaleidoscope - of emotions,
Feeling - out of focus,
I sit here - I

{I circle back-I circle back-I circle back-I circle back}

Dizzying - back to presence,
Dancing - with - this
Restlessness.
Weaving - a path back home and I

{I fall back, I fall back, I fall back, I fall back}

Further - a little further,
Uncertain - yet, willing
Until I glimpse
The Sky
Of my mind:
Cracked,
Wide,
Open.

*and exhaaaale*

There am I.

It doesn’t matter what this looks like, but how I feel when Spirit moves me through Art. Just like words are spells, I believe (and experience) all art to be the same.
I am engaging my whole being into bringing into the material world a current intention and whole presence.
In this session - and I have not done this in a while - I was embracing my desire to rebuild my foundation (wealth, financial health, business, etc.) and surrendering my desire to control.
While drawing, I will chant things that come to mind in each phase - this is usually a 3 step process: working at the very center (the “I”) moving outwards toward the world and my place in it.

The final outcome desired for each session is to transcend the blocks within, or at least start to crumble down any existing fears. Saying them out loud and releasing them during a session is how I begin to remove and improve.

I feel I may have narrowly averted a panic attack just now upon waking up. This rarely happens, I can only remember of one or two other times in past years, with similar feelings. 

I feel okay now. Everything is gonna be alright. I am not alone. I am loved.

When I woke up, I didn’t remember anything about my dreams but I awoke with an alertness or readiness. Sometimes I have a song brought to my attention clearly after waking and I immediately want to hear it on my phone. Today it was Fields of Gold from Sting. Classic.

Somewhere between waking and standing up to connect my phone to the charger, I started to feel a cave inside my chest opening up. A feeling of nothingness. Of beginning to fall “inside” slowly. Like nearly imploding in myself.

Between all that, and the way I found my thoughts navigating this weird experience I started tapping on my chest and breathing deeply. It took a few minutes of this but i eventually came back in to presence.

I remember a pull of wanting to feel it All, though. Of wanting to lose myself too. A strange sensation. But the memory of the last panic attack I had years ago came to mind and that was why I started to work on myself right there in my body.

This is such vulnerable posting but at the same time, I owe it to my future selves to leave this openly for her to read. Maybe she would need to read it again. Or anyone out there.

The answer is in the body. Not in the mind. In my breath. In my heartbeat. 

That is Everything.

📚Hugh Prather, Notes to Myself 

If you tell me the way you see it rather than the way it is, then this helps me to more fully discover the way I see it.

+

A generalization is an assertion that what is true for me is also true for not-me. I notice that I often talk in generalities to produce the illusion that my “truth” is shared. In this way, I pick up a little support (sameness) for my individualness and don’t feel so alone.

+

Sometimes, when I generalize, I am saying, “Let’s pretend I am God” and of course the other person argues that point endlessly. 

+

But I notice that if the other person takes a stand for himself and states his thoughts, as *his* thoughts, I pay more attention to what he is saying, and look deeper in myself.

~ H.P.

📚 Hugh Prather, Notes to Myself

At first, I thought that to “be myself” meant simply to act the way I feel. I would ask myself a question such as 

“What do I want to say to this person?”

And very often the answer was surprisingly negative.

🧵

It seemed that when I looked inside, the negative feelings were the ones I noticed first. Possibly I noticed them because of their social unusualness; possibly, they stood out because acting negatively was what I feared.

But I soon found that behind most negative feelings were deeper, more positive feelings – if I held still long enough to look. 

The more I attempted to “be me” the more “mes” I found there were.
I now see that “being me” means acknowledging all that I feel at the moment, and then taking responsibility for my actions by consciously choosing which level my feelings I am going to respond to.

@CleoOnTheRange Cleo what an amazing thing to know about you ❤️‍🔥 Artists and models alike understand what the job truly entails, there is deep respect in the community for it generally.

@CleoOnTheRange I got started here in Puerto Rico around 2018, and just began to ask a few of my artist friends around, letting them know I was available to try it out. It took a few months maybe, following the leads others gave me.

@CleoOnTheRange Then, one of those friends casually msgs me, asking if I can sub her that same evening for modeling for an artists’ group that got together weekly. I said yes 😅 - I had to keep telling myself I was ready, and that my recent research of art modeling was enough to begin.

@CleoOnTheRange And … It was an amazing first time I’ll never forget. 👌🏽☺️ 
After that, people started to reference me to other groups, for workshops or talk to professors. I modeled afterwards at art schools nearby. It’s the word-of-mouth that helps a lot with getting jobs in that field.

@CleoOnTheRange And engaging in that arena as well through art exhibitions, and experiences where artists meet. The beginning of breaking in might take a bit of time but once you’re in, it starts to flow a bit easier.

@boon_dLux 😆 This AMA is fun for me. 

So yes, I met him at one of the live drawing sessions* of an artist group. 

And yes, I did model at that particular session we met, from which we exchanged numbers**.

Me and the guy started talking / dating some months after that session we met at.

@boon_dLux Footnotes (added context for anyone unfamiliar with this field):

*Live drawing sessions can be private, or group based - tutored, untutored. They are held at institutions like art schools + can be held independently by seasoned artists who want to maintain their skills.

@boon_dLux **Exchange numbers:
- this is usual post session when navigating the social aspect aka “networking”, as artists would like the opportunity to work with you again or be able to refer you to others they know are looking.

@boon_dLux “I find it interesting how intentional nudity like that in a group setting is sort of anti-erotic”

YES 🙌🏽 I dunno how to begin responding to this bc it’s a deep rabbit hole 😄👌🏽 Art modeling has so many enigmatic aspects to it to delve deeper in …

@boon_dLux Yes yes and yes. There are also live drawing sessions that can be themed and specific to a certain niche of people interested in deep eros. What that looks like is always dependent on who hosts/guides the session.

@boon_dLux But setting that aside for now bc it’s a particular setting, I’ll come back to the part you mentioned about “intentional nudity”. This here is everything because without the guidelines of safety and etiquette of how to act within a live drawing session, then

@boon_dLux the parties involved cannot truly flourish: Artist in their craft, Model in their pose. Creativity can be stilted and block the flow of art and wonder, without the intention of respect for the model disrobed *and* of the artists intent to focus.

@boon_dLux Now, with the guidelines in place in the setting of a live drawing session, the term “erotic” itself falls away, in a sense. Almost like for the time the work is happening, the magic created there in between is all on its own realm.

@boon_dLux The model ceases, for a spell, to inhabit their body with their insecurities, mannerisms, voice, etc. 

Now, they are just pure form, volume, tones, light, shadow, chiaroscuro … it is definitely another way to experience our bodies in relationship to our nudity.
@boon_dLux Exactly ✨🗝️✨ ☺️

@strangestloop Important Q 🙏🏽 The way to go about it could vary depending on where you live + access to people involved in this field.

@strangestloop - IRL events: If you do not know artists already locally, start visiting some art galleries or exhibitions happening, or other events related to art 🖼️  
Talking to these people can generate more leads to follow.

@strangestloop - Academia: finding out which art schools are near you and seek out ways to apply for open job postings. E/school has their process of hiring, but occasionally you might get a prof who might hire you independently for their class and have their students chip in to pay the model.
@strangestloop My own experience 🌞 https://t.co/jql48r3JYh

@strangestloop Prob more to add when I think on this a little deeper. Your time will show up when it is meant to.

I had a “false start” yrs before I got truly involved modeling nude but it never panned bc for me, it didn’t “feel good”. Trust your gut and follow only what feels right for you.

@strangestloop Last but not least: many live drawing sessions seek model who work nude (or “disrobed/undraped”) bc it the best way to understand the body anatomy.

However, you can still work as a live model clothed as well and start there to get a feel for the effort involved. 🌞👌🏽

@satyrnian Ohh many faves - but this one comes up …

When I started getting into drum circles, I would bring my drums (cajón and djembe) to join. Sometimes though, the vibe was so tantalizingly good that I would have to tear myself from playing to dance.

@satyrnian So this one time I danced, I lost myself in it *completely*
It felt like I was telling the story of my journey and missing home. I’ve never forgotten that night since and the beautiful part was bc I broke through my anxiety of being seen by others.

@satyrnian It felt like getting lost in myself was exactly how I was supposed to be found.

And since then, I aim to remember again and again to bring myself past my edges. ❤️‍🔥

@DaveMeier Frustration:
Hard time communicating important feelings or thoughts - I feel like I am way deliberate w my thinking process and way of “accessing” my mind and memory. And concern over how to properly name things.

@DaveMeier There is smth intriguing about translating contents of mind into language as an autistic/adhd. I think this is why I love artistic and abstract practices that work away from language into visual/body. Aside, I still love writing bc it does help clarify my inner self in many ways.

@DaveMeier Something I’m proud of:
I took over a big part of a business I was working with in recent years - having committed to my role was not a decision I took lightly but I was invested in the company’s values and way of contributing ethically to consumers.

@DaveMeier I went through a number of shifts in mindset and behavioral as I got to know myself in new arenas I wasn’t used to. It was one of the most important learning experiences that I was grateful to have the chance to step into.

@DaveMeier Coming out of that role changed the way I see remote working, myself as a leader as I created frameworks and processes where there were none or minimal, and much more. 
I don’t often count that as a win but I think I should and feel freer to do today, thanks to your question. ☺️

So many tweets. Unfinished drafts. May not make it to the end of a sentence before I pull back asking 

What the heck am I trying to say?

I want to say everything. And everything is not enough. 
The process of unraveling knots is at times too tedious for the unruly, wild in me - a scream, a raucous laugh could say more than I can muster up the courage for.

Suddenly:

Maybe rather than describe what i want to “say” I can pivot to describing what I “see”?

Describe objectively, maybe even dispassionately, give it their names - build it up *without* overly identifying with.

All strangeness within
So that I may push against 
the entropy of
samethought—samemind.
Eager, to uncover
Faces I’ve never seen of Me,
embrace my wells of mystery.
She comes alive—
but I say, 
to Her
It is ok, 
stay Here
Love is no place to hide.

I don’t thank God enough for poetry. 

Poetry is my life source. My literal Life force - ever since I first learned what a rhyme was in 1st grade in Ms.Fritz’s class. Then I’ve become obsessed on/off about it over the years.

When proper speech fails me, I can take refuge in stacking words together like an art piece. To project an image that can begin to untangle some knot inside me - or maybe a lighthearted joy I yearn to amplify and share.

What it does for me, is so so much when I look back, that I could spend an eternity writing love letters to the Practice.

An eternity to love, of this one thing at least I could dare to be certain.

and then …

How will you CREATE space for future blessings to find their way into your Life?

Everything else is just grist for the mill.

There a nascent quietness I always experience after a crying release - the more powerful my release, the quieter I feel within afterwards. Like a huge cavern newly formed, waiting to be filled with something new.

Something fresh. Something true, unmasked. Something to show me the promise of life again. 
It’s already in *here*, of course, under persistent transmutation by my own hand and will.
Eternally revealing.

Loving myself consists of two things: forgiveness and fulfillment. 

When I forgive the parts of me that feel “lesser” than their stronger counterparts, I harmonize my inner nature, shadows and all, to create a sense of encompassing wholeness.

When I seek fulfillment, I honor the promises I make intentionally for myself to grow in love, care and self respect. My word is my power. Being deeply fulfilled is how I honor who I am and am Becoming in my quest for a fuller, well lived life on my terms.

Forgive consistently. 
Fulfill persistently.

@iam_mariapaula - Headphones / audio support are a must. (I believe I have audio processing disorder). 
Just got a new set this week and noticed immediate difference in my flow and mood. This includes earplugs, as alternative.
@iam_mariapaula Where I live now is significantly better noise wise than last location, but daily environment still hampers focus to varying degrees, depends on what kinds of tasks I’m doing.

@iam_mariapaula - Similarly, access to noise canceling features on my phone and/or alternative to hear preferences of music for focus. 

Noise canceling can help me greatly if I’m having a fragile kind of day and need to soothe my mind a bit, provide rest from daily stimuli on breaks.

@iam_mariapaula - Places to perform tasks: I have bunch of different little nooks to sit down when needed. I have an actual office desk but don’t always stay there. 
(I move pretty frequently but also *allow* myself to move freely)
@iam_mariapaula And standing desks are LEGIT when I feel like standing, also depending on type of task (I stacked two stools together so that works for now).

Often, a habit or a practice that held me through intense cycles might silently slip away from my daily life. 

I don’t notice how skipping “just this once” becomes its own permission slip to strip a valuable habit of its value, bit by bit, every time I’ve said

“No, not today.”

Coming back to mantra practice after a long while, I felt an immediate sense of coming Home. 

This felt Real. 

Something felt warm, inviting and was smiling with me. For me. Through me. 

So I softly chanted and slipped into this familiar feeling, toes in the river of change: https://t.co/loVxEIeR5i

The noisy trash disposal truck came and went, 

The honking car that arrives at my neighbor’s every morning came and went,

The neurotic in me questioning my choices came and went,

The desire to stop and move away to the next shiny object came and went …

Life is made of Comings and Goings:

love … sudden connections … insights … discomfort … pain … suffering … longing … hunger … satiety …

What was I holding onto during this steady pendulum of change? 

Did it truly anchor me *in between* it All?

My eyes were not meant to capture Everything - yet my gaze, engaged, is a soft reckoning of all I *sense* passes through me. A steady practice.

This is how I can best allow the All to flow, without capturing to turn into a desire - into a need - into a pattern - into a story …

Haven’t I seen IT yet? 

If truly meant to stay in my current reality (for longer than a glimpse), I can learn to hold it lightly and dance with it.

Miss Honey’s backstory is one of loss and deep disconnection w the world around her since childhood. She was v misunderstood by others, even while she could pierce illusions and false realities but others couldn’t see into her.

@veggiebat_ @moonlitstates As a deeply nourishing sensitive who could’ve developed her gifts further (and even embrace the raw power of her divine femenine nature), she gradually lost this access under pressure to conform within family, school and other formative social structures she grew up in.

@veggiebat_ @moonlitstates Having established independence as an adult and a good-enough peace in her simple yet mesmerizing cottage out in the woods, she is content with her life thus far. She enjoys cultivating the richness of her students spirits. Life is decent, life is good.

@veggiebat_ @moonlitstates Then Matilda comes into her class and something about her stirs these memories of true spiritual and magical loss. Sensing the extraordinary in Matilda, she develops a further closeness to her, viewing her own life mysteriously repeated in Matilda:

@veggiebat_ @moonlitstates her sensitivity and magic being ignored/downplayed/punished in her home, feeling separate from others, not knowing how to use her gifts without becoming a target … Matilda is her (Miss Honey) all over again.

@veggiebat_ @moonlitstates Only this time, she feels she has a serious role to play, understanding that if she doesn’t help Matilda somehow, to flourish as the powerful magical being she is growing into, then it will surely be lost …

@veggiebat_ @moonlitstates … suppressed along the way, eventually forgotten in a dusty spirituality barren world stepping its heavy boots on anything edging towards the strange and unknown.

Matilda is the child she always wanted. Miss Honey is the nurturing Mother Matilda always deserved. 💗🌷💗

I am 
full of fallen pieces

As the newest contours of love,

Ever changing.
Fresh beginning.
A tribute to the Olden.

the skillful gaze
Of returned wonder.

To come back, to me. 
Each love, made whole again, 

— transforming.

Listening.

in judgment

with kindness?

of more profound shifts 
within, to then be met with
the world,

As you truly were
All along.

others are mirror to this journey inside ourselves 
but don’t mistake that
for outsourcing habitually the 
Real-ness with which 
you must see yourself 
first.
@GregoryHansell 🙌🏽

You’re holding on to what’s familiar and no longer comfortable. 

You’re open to seek advice from others yet don’t remember the sound of your own true voice.

You’re afraid to be bold and cast waves but see others transcend their own limits wondering “Could that ever be me.”

For everything you say Yes to, you are also saying No to something else. 

Could it be an old dream gathering dust, forgotten? Whatever it may be for you, if it’s coming from your heart it will keep knocking on your door to seek its maximum expression through you.

Your body. 
A temporary gift.

And until you become aware of how, and what, you are (be)living, it will prove much harder to stretch the boundaries from where you are to where you want to be.
Pay attention to what you consume - if it has no lasting value, it will end up consuming you.

That looks different yet

Has only One true name.

So simple, yet a powerful design.

bloom in all its rightful places

from Love.

how do I love what they are when I feel the distance of what could be?

Depths are a matter of facing one’s own heaven and hell—
without that,

drought.

increasing my energy,
refining precision swinging a machete,

removing deadgrowth,

downloading insights from Mother

and a nice, ice cold drink.

text:
The urge to become unhinged and see what cracks open within.
Who is appalled, who sticks around for more. 
What becomes relevant and what becomes dissolved with barely a trace. 

I want to twist these insides open and coax others into a conversation that leaves us spellbound.
